meta.originalTitle: The nature of Yawning 打哈欠的本质

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

While fatigue, drowsiness or boredom easily bring on yawns, scientists are discovering there is more to yawning than most people think. Not much is known about why we yawn or if it serves any useful function. People have already learned that yawning can be infectious. “Contagious yawning” is the increase in likelihood that you will yawn after watching or hearing someone else yawn, but not much is known about the underlying causes, and very little research has been done on the subject. However, scientists at the University at Albany, as well as the University of Leeds and the University of London, have done some exploration.

It is commonly believed that people yawn as a result of being sleepy or tired or because they need oxygen. However, the latest research shows that a yawn can help cool the brain and help it work more effectively, which is quite different from the popular belief that yawning promotes sleep and is a sign of tiredness. Dr Andrew Gallup and his colleagues at the University at Albany in New York State said their experiments on 44 students showed that raising or lowering oxygen and carbon-dioxide levels in the blood did not produce that reaction. In the study, participants were shown videos of people laughing and yawning, and researchers counted how many times the volunteers responded to the “contagious yawns.” The researchers found that those who breathed through the nose rather than the mouth were less likely to yawn when watching a video of other people yawning. The same effect was found among those who held a cool pack to their forehead, whereas those who held a warm pack yawned more while watching the video. Since yawning occurs when brain temperature rises, sending cool blood to the brain serves to maintain optimal levels of mental efficiency.

Yawning is universal to humans and many animals. Cats, dogs and fish yawn just like humans do, but they yawn spontaneously. Only humans and chimpanzees, our closest relatives in the animal kingdom, have shown definite contagious yawning. Though much of yawning is due to suggestibility, sometimes people do not need to actually see a person yawn to involuntarily yawn themselves: hearing someone yawning or even reading about yawning can cause the same reaction.

However, contagious yawning goes beyond mere suggestibility. Recent studies show that contagious yawning is also related to our predisposition toward empathy — the ability to understand and connect with others' emotional states. So empathy is important, sure, but how could it possibly be related to contagious yawning? Psychologists at Leeds University in England set out to answer that. In their study, researchers selected 40 psychology students and 40 engineering students. Generally, psychology students are more likely to feel empathy for others, while engineering students are thought to be concerned with objects and science. Each student was made to wait individually in a waiting room along with an undercover assistant who yawned ten times in as many minutes. The students were then given an emotional-quotient test: they were shown 40 images of eyes and asked what emotion each one displayed. The results support the idea that contagious yawning is linked to empathy. The psychology students, whose future profession requires them to focus on others, yawned contagiously an average of 5.5 times in the waiting room and scored 28 out of 40 on the emotional test. The engineering students, who tend to focus on numbers and systems, yawned an average of 1.5 times and scored 25.5 out of 40. The difference does not sound large, but researchers consider it significant. Strangely enough, women — generally viewed as more emotionally attuned — did not score any higher than men.

Another study, led by Atsushi Senju, a cognitive researcher at the University of London, also sought to answer that question. People with autism spectrum disorder are considered to be developmentally impaired emotionally: they have trouble connecting with others and find it difficult to feel empathy. Since autistics have difficulty feeling empathy, they should not be susceptible to contagious yawning. To find out, Senju and his colleagues placed 49 children aged 7 to 15 in a room with a television. Twenty-four of the subjects had been diagnosed with autism spectrum disorder; the other 25 were non-autistic. The children were shown short clips of people yawning as well as clips of people opening their mouths but not yawning. While the kids with autism showed the same lack of reaction to both kinds of clips, the non-autistic kids yawned more after the clips of people yawning.

There have also been studies suggesting that yawning, especially psychological “contagious” yawning, may have developed as a way of keeping a group of animals alert and bonding members of a group into a more unified one. If an animal is drowsy or bored, it may not be as alert as it should be to spring into action, and its yawning is practically saying, “Hey, I need some rest; you stay awake.” Therefore, a contagious yawn could be an instinctual reaction to a signal from one member of the herd reminding the others to stay alert when danger comes. Thus, the theory provides evidence that yawning comes from the evolution of early humans who needed to be ready to physically exert themselves at any given moment.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
, in boxes 1–5 on your answer sheet. You may use any letter more than once.
Research shows that yawning is closely related to occupations.
An overview of the latest research on yawning.
Yawning is used to regulate brain temperature.
Scientists discovered some evidence disproving the earlier understanding of yawning.

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 1. 段落匹配（Questions 1–5：Which paragraph contains the following information?）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：人类的想象会引发打哈欠。
答案：C
解析：定位 Paragraph C 中 “sometimes people do not need to actually see a person yawn to involuntarily yawn themselves: hearing someone yawning or even reading about yawning can cause the same reaction”，“reading about yawning” 需通过 “想象” 打哈欠场景引发反应，与题干 “想象引发打哈欠” 对应，因此答案为 C。
题目翻译：研究表明打哈欠与职业密切相关。
答案：D
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “40 psychology students... 40 engineering students... The psychology students... yawned contagiously an average of 5.5 times... The engineering students... yawned an average of 1.5 times”，研究将 “专业（未来职业取向）” 与打哈欠次数关联，证明职业与打哈欠相关，与题干对应，因此答案为 D。
题目翻译：打哈欠最新研究概述。
答案：A
解析：定位 Paragraph A 中 “scientists at the University at Albany, as well as the University of Leeds and the University of London, have done some exploration”，总览三所大学的最新研究方向，属于 “研究概述”，与题干对应，因此答案为 A。
题目翻译：打哈欠用于调节大脑温度。
答案：B
解析：定位 Paragraph B 中 “a yawn can help cool the brain and help it work more effectively... since yawning occurs when brain temperature rises, sending cool blood to the brain serves to maintain optimal levels of mental efficiency”，明确提到打哈欠通过降温调节大脑温度，与题干对应，因此答案为 B。
题目翻译：科学家发现反驳此前打哈欠认知的证据。
答案：B
解析：定位 Paragraph B 中 “It is commonly believed that people yawn as a result of being sleepy or tired or because they need oxygen. However, the latest research shows that a yawn can help cool the brain... Dr Andrew Gallup... experiments on 44 students showed that raising or lowering oxygen and carbon-dioxide levels in the blood did not produce that reaction”，实验证明 “缺氧不引发打哈欠”，反驳了 “打哈欠源于缺氧” 的旧观点，与题干对应，因此答案为 B。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：Humans’ imaginations can cause yawning.
题目翻译：人类的想象会引发打哈欠。
答案：C
解析：定位 Paragraph C 中 “sometimes people do not need to actually see a person yawn to involuntarily yawn themselves: hearing someone yawning or even reading about yawning can cause the same reaction”，“reading about yawning” 需通过 “想象” 打哈欠场景引发反应，与题干 “想象引发打哈欠” 对应，因此答案为 C。

（2）题目 2：Research shows that yawning is closely related to occupations.
题目翻译：研究表明打哈欠与职业密切相关。
答案：D
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “40 psychology students... 40 engineering students... The psychology students... yawned contagiously an average of 5.5 times... The engineering students... yawned an average of 1.5 times”，研究将 “专业（未来职业取向）” 与打哈欠次数关联，证明职业与打哈欠相关，与题干对应，因此答案为 D。

（3）题目 3：An overview of the latest research on yawning.
题目翻译：打哈欠最新研究概述。
答案：A
解析：定位 Paragraph A 中 “scientists at the University at Albany, as well as the University of Leeds and the University of London, have done some exploration”，总览三所大学的最新研究方向，属于 “研究概述”，与题干对应，因此答案为 A。

（4）题目 4：Yawning is used to regulate brain temperature.
题目翻译：打哈欠用于调节大脑温度。
答案：B
解析：定位 Paragraph B 中 “a yawn can help cool the brain and help it work more effectively... since yawning occurs when brain temperature rises, sending cool blood to the brain serves to maintain optimal levels of mental efficiency”，明确提到打哈欠通过降温调节大脑温度，与题干对应，因此答案为 B。

（5）题目 5：Scientists discovered some evidence disproving the earlier understanding of yawning.
题目翻译：科学家发现反驳此前打哈欠认知的证据。
答案：B
解析：定位 Paragraph B 中 “It is commonly believed that people yawn as a result of being sleepy or tired or because they need oxygen. However, the latest research shows that a yawn can help cool the brain... Dr Andrew Gallup... experiments on 44 students showed that raising or lowering oxygen and carbon-dioxide levels in the blood did not produce that reaction”，实验证明 “缺氧不引发打哈欠”，反驳了 “打哈欠源于缺氧” 的旧观点，与题干对应，因此答案为 B。

children.text: Match each of the following research results with the university from which it comes.
You may use any letter more than once.
There is no gender difference in the cause of yawning.
People with certain disorders are less likely to be affected by other people yawning.
Yawning is associated with the way people breathe.
People who are trained to feel empathy for others are more likely to yawn than those who are untrained.

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 2. 研究结果 - 大学匹配（Questions 6–9：Match each research result with the university）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：打哈欠的诱因不存在性别差异。
答案：B（University of Leeds，利兹大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “Strangely enough, women - generally viewed as more emotionally attuned - did not score any higher than men”，利兹大学的实验发现女性打哈欠相关得分未高于男性，即 “无性别差异”，因此答案为 B。
题目翻译：患特定疾病的人不易受他人打哈欠影响。
答案：C（University of London，伦敦大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph E 中 “People with autism spectrum disorder... have trouble connecting with others and find it difficult to feel empathy... the kids with autism showed the same lack of reaction to both kinds of clips”，伦敦大学的研究发现自闭症儿童（患特定障碍）不易被传染性打哈欠影响，因此答案为 C。
题目翻译：打哈欠与人们的呼吸方式相关。
答案：A（University at Albany，奥尔巴尼大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph B 中 “the researchers found that those who breathed through the nose rather than the mouth were less likely to yawn when watching a video of other people yawning”，奥尔巴尼大学的实验发现 “鼻呼吸者比嘴呼吸者更不易被传染打哈欠”，证明呼吸方式与打哈欠相关，因此答案为 A。
题目翻译：接受过共情训练的人比未接受训练的人更易打哈欠。
答案：B（University of Leeds，利兹大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “psychology students, whose future profession requires them to focus on others, yawned contagiously an average of 5.5 times... engineering students, who tend to focus on numbers and systems, yawned an average of 1.5 times”，利兹大学的实验显示 “需共情的心理学学生比侧重系统的工程学学生更易打哈欠”，即 “接受共情训练者更易打哈欠”，因此答案为 B。

items.text:
（1）题目 6：There is no gender difference in the cause of yawning.
题目翻译：打哈欠的诱因不存在性别差异。
答案：B（University of Leeds，利兹大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “Strangely enough, women - generally viewed as more emotionally attuned - did not score any higher than men”，利兹大学的实验发现女性打哈欠相关得分未高于男性，即 “无性别差异”，因此答案为 B。

（2）题目 7：People with certain disorders are less likely to be affected by other people yawning.
题目翻译：患特定疾病的人不易受他人打哈欠影响。
答案：C（University of London，伦敦大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph E 中 “People with autism spectrum disorder... have trouble connecting with others and find it difficult to feel empathy... the kids with autism showed the same lack of reaction to both kinds of clips”，伦敦大学的研究发现自闭症儿童（患特定障碍）不易被传染性打哈欠影响，因此答案为 C。

（3）题目 8：Yawning is associated with the way people breathe.
题目翻译：打哈欠与人们的呼吸方式相关。
答案：A（University at Albany，奥尔巴尼大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph B 中 “the researchers found that those who breathed through the nose rather than the mouth were less likely to yawn when watching a video of other people yawning”，奥尔巴尼大学的实验发现 “鼻呼吸者比嘴呼吸者更不易被传染打哈欠”，证明呼吸方式与打哈欠相关，因此答案为 A。

（4）题目 9：People who are trained to feel empathy for others are more likely to yawn than those who are untrained.
题目翻译：接受过共情训练的人比未接受训练的人更易打哈欠。
答案：B（University of Leeds，利兹大学）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “psychology students, whose future profession requires them to focus on others, yawned contagiously an average of 5.5 times... engineering students, who tend to focus on numbers and systems, yawned an average of 1.5 times”，利兹大学的实验显示 “需共情的心理学学生比侧重系统的工程学学生更易打哈欠”，即 “接受共情训练者更易打哈欠”，因此答案为 B。

children.text: from the passage for each answer.
Another theory shows that yawning is used for
individuals into a tighter social unit. Alternatively, yawning can help increase alertness of group members in case
is close. For example, yawning signals that a member of the group needs some
and requires the others to stay aware of the surrounding situation. This theory proves that yawning is only a spontaneous behaviour resulting from some part of a simple

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：另一理论表明，打哈欠用于将个体_\___\__成更紧密的社会群体。
答案：bonding（凝聚）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “yawning... may have developed as a way of keeping a group of animals alert and bonding members of a group into a more unified one”，“bonding... into a more unified one” 对应 “\___\___ individuals into a tighter social unit”，因此答案为 “bonding”。
题目翻译：此外，打哈欠可帮助提高群体成员的警觉性，以防_\___\__临近。
答案：danger（危险）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “reminding the others to stay alert when danger comes”，“in case \___\___ is close” 对应 “when danger comes”，因此答案为 “danger”。
题目翻译：例如，打哈欠信号表明某群体成员需要_\___\__，并要求其他人留意周围情况。
答案：rest（休息）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “its yawning is practically saying, ‘Hey, I need some rest; you stay awake’”，明确提到打哈欠信号表明需要 “休息”，因此答案为 “rest”。
题目翻译：该理论证明，打哈欠仅是早期人类简单_\___\__系统某部分引发的自发行为。
答案：alert（警觉）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “reminding the others to stay alert when danger comes”，打哈欠源于 “保持警觉” 的需求，即属于 “警觉系统”，因此答案为 “alert”。

items.text:
（1）题目 10：Another theory shows that yawning is used for \___\___ individuals into a tighter social unit.
题目翻译：另一理论表明，打哈欠用于将个体_\___\__成更紧密的社会群体。
答案：bonding（凝聚）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “yawning... may have developed as a way of keeping a group of animals alert and bonding members of a group into a more unified one”，“bonding... into a more unified one” 对应 “\___\___ individuals into a tighter social unit”，因此答案为 “bonding”。

（2）题目 11：Alternatively, yawning can help increase alertness of group members in case \___\___ is close.
题目翻译：此外，打哈欠可帮助提高群体成员的警觉性，以防_\___\__临近。
答案：danger（危险）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “reminding the others to stay alert when danger comes”，“in case \___\___ is close” 对应 “when danger comes”，因此答案为 “danger”。

（3）题目 12：For example, yawning signals that a member of the group needs some \___\___ and requires the others to stay aware of the surrounding situation.
题目翻译：例如，打哈欠信号表明某群体成员需要_\___\__，并要求其他人留意周围情况。
答案：rest（休息）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “its yawning is practically saying, ‘Hey, I need some rest; you stay awake’”，明确提到打哈欠信号表明需要 “休息”，因此答案为 “rest”。

（4）题目 13：This theory proves that yawning is only a spontaneous behaviour resulting from some part of a simple \___\___ system in early humans.
题目翻译：该理论证明，打哈欠仅是早期人类简单_\___\__系统某部分引发的自发行为。
答案：alert（警觉）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “reminding the others to stay alert when danger comes”，打哈欠源于 “保持警觉” 的需求，即属于 “警觉系统”，因此答案为 “alert”。
